item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. It's just kind of there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this idea. I want to like this scip. But it feels so…pointless. It just feels like there could have been more to this story, and I wish there was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this remind anyone else of 2338?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Alexus-0, while it's great that you seem to be endeavoring to comment on every SCP article you read, it might be good to slow down a bit with your posting so you can use proper grammar and capitalization. People will be more likely to respond to a comment that looks thoughtfully-written rather than rushed out in a hurry, and also it'll be less likely to be interpreted as spam.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: maybe im missing something but i cant really see why there keeping this guy around. so he can't be shot, big whoop throw him in an acid vat then. I get the foundation only actively seeks to neutralise meter class stuff but this guy has shown to be dangerous and hard to stop, why take the risk?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doc Ock wannabe

item.title: Re: Second adendum needs a rewrite
item.content:encoded:
I would actually recommend quotes around "a lot." The foundation only has SCP-070's record of how much it was, and it's probably how he'd talk. Maybe a footnote: "Urine samples indicate SCP-070 had consumed more than [digits] kilograms of Peyote."

I'm more concerned about the object class. Humanoid/sapient is usually auto-Euclid, and this one has breached containment once.

item.title: Second adendum needs a rewrite
item.content:encoded: " the wings and chains of SCP-070 are as strong as high-quality alloy steel. However, they are also as dense as steel, and SCP-070 cannot move about as a normal human due to the weight of its wings." could use a rewrite as well as the "a lot" measurement of the peyote. "A substantial amount" or "Quantities exceeding" perhaps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As this stands right now, the SCP feels a bit pointless, with no follow-up or development.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An oldie and a goodie, but it hasn't aged all that well tonally and I'd suggest that someone gives it a an edit or rewrite to fix the tone and bring it up to scratch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "large amounts/quantities of" would sound a lot better to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, a lot sounds like the right measurement. You start off with a little bit of anything good and it turns into a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Final addendum could use some rewording. I recognize that the Foundation has no way of knowing the exact quantity of peyote this individual smoked, but "a lot of peyote" feels a little bit off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I forgot about this one. It's pretty good.

This seems to be Euclid, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thats scary! My street name is Kiowa St. No lie, Im serious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the "sleep-walking" part +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stand corrected. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sticky_foam for those interested. Sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems to actually be called "sticky foam" though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP, but I have one nitpick with the tone. The use of "sticky-foam guns" seems to rather contrast with the overall clinical tone of the entry. Anyone have a more technical description of "sticky-foam?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just the concept of the wings made this one cool for me… kind-of like metal angel wings, with a mind of their own. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this one a lot, particularly in how it turns the presumptions of "dude with wings" on their head. also the order of the signs of distress - quantifiable, clinical measures first, human suffering last.

the tone/wording of the addendum is a bit rough, though. i'd expect "is named" to be "claims to be named," and the bit about peyote feels like it should be phrased more formally, or included in the sentence as a quote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any reason why they can't subdue 070, surgically remove the wings, and lock them up for further study?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a decent SCP. Not great, but decent. The line about the peyote made me smile enough to decide to upvote it.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded:
No, this is the perfect place. LBD, you were permabanned. You whined enough that we agreed to review it in one month. After 24 hours, you evaded your ban. Your right to a review was revoked.

tl:dr - GTFO.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded:
Um… I never tried to evade my ban. Seriously.

But this prolly isn't the place for this conversation.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: No, it was that your behaviour was to be reviewed in a month. And, since you tried to evade your ban, you were put on perma.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: Actualy, clef said one month. I have it in my logs

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: No one eversaid anything about one month LBD. It was a perma ban from the get go. Deal.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: Oh? You got banned as well? Good. Anyway, I'm doing some work on editing a few of the ones that are up for reveiw. You have a nice day, I'm going to go be productive for once.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded:
Hey, LBD, lets whine about being banned!

Oh, wait, no, I don't want to do that. It would be obnoxious.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded:
's good by me. It's kinda hard to get involved with a comunity when I'm not chatting real time with them though, you know?

Anyway it was supposed to be a one month ban… and my IP address has changed since then…

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I give permission

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: Honestly, "better" was what I was immediately shooting for, just to get it off Death Row. "Great" can happen when there aren't 50 articles to review for the Mass Edit….

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: Hmm… seems like it deals with some of the problems of it being "cool," so that's good. I like the edit, though I'd want to check with the original author if he can be contacted. Good luck with the edit.

item.title: Re: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded: It's much better, certainly, but needs a bit of polish.

item.title: Proposed revision
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-eval.pastebin.com/m5d6086bd

This is the first draft of my proposed revision for SCP-070. I'm not totally satisfied with it, but I think (I hope) I kept the basic idea intact. Feedback welcome as always.

I'll leave it in Pastebin till a Mod or Admin gives the go-ahead to update the entry. Right now I'm going to bed and I'll look for comments in the morning.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
Ideally, I think this was intended as a direct subversion of Xman type SCPs. The guy has metal wings. That sounds awesome. But… he can't fly, they can't be removed, and they trap him by anchoring him to concrete.

I see the idea.

But it's written in such a way that the blatant badfic potential is highlighted here, and I think that's part of the problem. The whole bit about the wings reacting to his mood, or blocking bullets? Yeh. Giving the wings an obvious benefit ruins the type subversion.

I almost like this SCP, and I'm glad it's up for edit, because with a few changes it could be a solid entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So uh, the dude has metal wings.

I think this another case of 'this interesting, cool, and I don't understand why it's an SCP'

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Only from chat.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Didn't he get banned?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one was written by LBD, who hasn't been around in a couple of months. If he doesn't come back to save it, I'll try to fix it up myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy poo! When I was writing my last comment, people popped up and commented before me! It must be pretty busy right now.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Well, even though what Kendrakirai was a little…. weird, I agree that a there are a lot of good ones up for deletion. And if an SCP is positively rated, especially one that has 24 points, then some people consider it good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This entry has been reviewed and slated for editing. If you are the original author, please review this entry and comments, and see what changes could be made. Other users may edit ONLY:

  1. If the original author cannot be found/contacted
  2. If the original author gives permission

If no editing has been done on this entry after a period of two weeks, this entry will come under review for deletion. If you have any questions, please contact a mod or admin.

Start Date: 9/11/09
End Date: 9/25/09

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Psst, kendra, check the current list of articles under review. There were a few on the list that surprised me, but like Bright said, popular does not necessarily mean good. Shouldn't take you long to think of several things that are very popular but are utter crap….

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Just because it's positively rated, doesn't mean it's good.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
I'm trying to. I'm trying to find some explanation for his….weirdness tonight. I mean, worried that a +24 rated page is up for deletion? Mass Edit or no, I'm preeetty sure that's safe.

I dunno though..maybe I'm just a bit off myself tonight…

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Now now son, play nice.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
Again, it's a +7 rating.

Is today Opposite Day for you or something, Miniblini? 'cause you're not making any sense at all. Are you drunk? Stoned? Just decided to act like an idiot? Trolling? Seriously, there must be something going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DON'T GET RID!!!

I like this one, because it sorta gets a message out there, don't do peyote, or you'll get fucked. I might be pretty biased though, as I am Native American, so it is always nice to read about one if my own.

well, if you do get rid of this one, I will be sad, But then I will probably post suggestions in the writers block forum Specifically related to Native Americans. Seriously people, Native mythology is so cool, you don't even KNOW! XD

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: I will attempt to fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing of the addenda on this SCP is kinda… rough is the best way to put it, I think. Clinical tone does not require short, choppy sentences.
